1.energy: Initiation, fresh starts, planting seeds, independence, courage
1.description: This is your year of new beginnings and fresh starts. The Universal Year 1 energy supports taking initiative and launching new ventures. This is the beginning of a new 9-year cycle in your life, making it the perfect time to plant seeds for what you want to grow over the coming years. The energy favors bold action, independence, and pioneering new territories in your life. What you start now will develop and unfold throughout the entire cycle ahead.
1.guidance: This is your year to START. Don't wait for perfect conditions or others' permission - act on your inspiration and trust your instincts. Focus on what YOU want to create, not what others expect. This is not a year for collaboration or compromise - it's about establishing your individual path. Be willing to stand alone if necessary. Plant many seeds through new projects, relationships, or directions. Take initiative in all areas where you want change.
avoid: Staying stuck in old patterns or relationships that have run their course
Waiting for others to give permission or approval
Starting things half-heartedly without full commitment
Being overly dependent on others' opinions or support
Hesitating or second-guessing your impulses

monthlyFocus.early: Clarify your vision and set intentions for the entire 9-year cycle ahead
monthlyFocus.middle: Take bold action on at least one major new beginning
monthlyFocus.late: Review what you've started and recommit to seeing it through

1.affirmation: I courageously initiate new beginnings aligned with my authentic desires.

2.energy: Gestation, cooperation, details emerging, sensitivity, diplomacy
2.description: This is your year of patience and partnership. After the bold initiations of Year 1, Year 2 requires a completely different energy - one of allowing rather than forcing, cooperating rather than dominating, and tending to details rather than rushing ahead. The seeds you planted last year are germinating beneath the surface. You can't see much progress yet, but crucial development is happening. This year emphasizes relationships, collaboration, and the careful, detailed work that supports future growth.
2.guidance: Practice patience - what you started last year is developing but not yet ready to emerge. This is not a year for forcing outcomes or making aggressive moves. Instead, focus on cooperation, building relationships, and handling the details that will support your vision. Your role is to water the seeds, not dig them up to check progress. Pay attention to partnerships - who are your allies and how can you collaborate? Work on the small, detailed tasks that seem insignificant but create strong foundations. Trust the process even when you can't see results.
avoid: Forcing outcomes or being impatient with natural timing
Trying to go it alone instead of seeking collaboration
Ignoring details or being sloppy with small tasks
Being overly sensitive to others' opinions or criticism
Isolation - this is a partnership year
Making major changes or starting new projects - continue what you started

monthlyFocus.early: Identify key relationships and potential partnerships
monthlyFocus.middle: Tend to details and background work that supports your vision
monthlyFocus.late: Strengthen diplomatic skills and practice patience

2.affirmation: I trust the process and cooperate with divine timing and right partnerships.

3.energy: Growth, expansion, creativity, joy, social connection, communication
3.description: This is your year of creative expression and joyful expansion. After the patience of Year 2, Year 3 brings an outpouring of creative energy and the first visible signs of growth from what you planted in Year 1. This year emphasizes self-expression, social connection, and creativity in all forms. Your project or vision is beginning to take recognizable form, and it's time to share it with others. The energy is lighter, more playful, and socially oriented than the previous two years. This is your year to communicate, create, and celebrate.
3.guidance: Express yourself! This is the year to share your vision, ideas, and creative work with others. Don't hold back - communicate enthusiastically about what you're building. Engage your creative abilities whether through writing, speaking, art, or any form of expression. Expand your social network and connect with people who inspire you. Say yes to social invitations and opportunities to share your work. Allow yourself joy and playfulness - creativity flows best in a light, fun energy. This is also an excellent year for any form of creative project, artistic pursuit, or expressive communication.
avoid: Suppressing your creative impulses or playing small
Isolation or withdrawing from social opportunities
Taking things too seriously or being overly practical
Scattering your energy across too many projects
Negative self-talk or criticism that dampens creative flow
Hiding your light or being afraid to share your work

monthlyFocus.early: Open creative channels through any form of artistic expression
monthlyFocus.middle: Share your vision and work with expanding social circles
monthlyFocus.late: Celebrate progress and cultivate joy in the creative process

3.affirmation: I joyfully express my creativity and share my gifts with the world.

4.energy: Hard work, discipline, structure, stability, practical implementation
4.description: This is your year of foundation building and practical implementation. After the expansive creativity of Year 3, Year 4 requires grounding your vision in solid, practical reality. This is the most work-intensive year of the cycle - it's time to roll up your sleeves and do the hard, disciplined work of building strong foundations. What you create this year will support everything that follows, so cutting corners now will cause problems later. The energy emphasizes structure, systems, routine, and practical details. This isn't the most exciting year, but it's perhaps the most important for long-term success.
4.guidance: Commit to the hard work. Create systems, establish routines, and build solid foundations in all areas related to your Year 1 intentions. This is not a year for shortcuts or quick wins - it's about patient, methodical building. Focus on practical implementation over vision or creativity. Establish discipline and structure. Create the organizational systems that will support your goals. Handle all the practical details - finances, legal matters, logistics, health foundations. Be willing to do unglamorous work. Your future success depends on the foundations you build now.
avoid: Looking for shortcuts or quick fixes
Being sloppy with details or cutting corners
Changing direction or starting new projects - this is a year to build on existing foundations
Expecting excitement or immediate rewards
Neglecting practical matters in favor of vision or creativity
Giving up because the work feels too hard or boring

monthlyFocus.early: Create systems and structures for your primary goals
monthlyFocus.middle: Commit to disciplined daily action and routine
monthlyFocus.late: Review and strengthen any weak spots in your foundations

4.affirmation: I build strong foundations through disciplined effort and practical action.

5.energy: Unexpected change, freedom, adventure, expansion, new experiences
5.description: This is your year of change, freedom, and expansion. After the hard work and discipline of Year 4, Year 5 brings a complete energy shift toward movement, variety, and new experiences. This is the most dynamic and unpredictable year of the cycle. Expect the unexpected - changes may come from outside your control, or you may feel driven to create change yourself. The foundations you built last year now support greater freedom and adventure. This year emphasizes growth through experience, travel, new connections, and willingness to embrace change rather than resist it.
5.guidance: Embrace change and seek new experiences. This is your year to expand beyond familiar territories - travel, meet new people, try new things, take calculated risks. Don't cling to security or routine. Be adaptable and flexible when unexpected changes occur (and they will). This is an excellent year for marketing, networking, sales, or any activity that involves connecting with diverse people and situations. Say yes to adventure and opportunities outside your normal comfort zone. Allow yourself freedom from routines established in Year 4. The key is balance - enough structure to be productive, enough freedom to grow.
avoid: Resisting change or clinging to security
Staying stuck in routines that have become stale
Playing it too safe or avoiding all risk
Isolation or limiting yourself to familiar people and places
Excess and addiction - the shadow side of freedom
Impulsiveness without any consideration of consequences

monthlyFocus.early: Identify areas where you crave more freedom or change
monthlyFocus.middle: Take action on new experiences and expanded connections
monthlyFocus.late: Integrate lessons from changes and prepare for next phase

5.affirmation: I embrace change and expand my life through new experiences and authentic freedom.

6.energy: Duty, family, service, love, harmony, domestic matters, decision-making
6.description: This is your year of responsibility, service, and deepening love. After the freedom and expansion of Year 5, Year 6 calls you back to matters of home, family, and personal relationships. This year emphasizes duty and service, particularly to those close to you. Important decisions often arise this year, especially regarding relationships and domestic matters. The energy encourages creating beauty, harmony, and healing in your immediate environment. You may feel called to take on more responsibility for others' well-being. This is the heart of the cycle - a year focused on love, both giving and receiving.
6.guidance: Embrace responsibility and deepen your capacity for love and service. This is often a year when relationship commitments deepen - marriages, births, or taking on care for family members. Create beauty and harmony in your home environment. Be willing to serve those you love without martyrdom. Make important decisions from the heart rather than the head. Focus on quality time with family and loved ones. This is also an excellent year for healing - both giving healing to others and receiving it yourself. Balance service with self-care.
avoid: Martyrdom or sacrificing yourself excessively
Interfering in others' lives under the guise of helping
Perfectionism or having unrealistic expectations of loved ones
Avoiding responsibility when it's truly yours to carry
Making major decisions from obligation rather than love
Neglecting self-care while attending to others

monthlyFocus.early: Assess relationships and domestic matters requiring attention
monthlyFocus.middle: Make important decisions with both heart and practical wisdom
monthlyFocus.late: Create beauty and harmony in your environment and relationships

6.affirmation: I embrace loving responsibility and create harmony in all my relationships.

7.energy: Introspection, spiritual growth, analysis, rest, solitude, inner development
7.description: This is your year of inner work and spiritual development. After the relational intensity of Year 6, Year 7 turns inward for contemplation, study, and spiritual growth. This is the most introspective year of the cycle - a time for rest, reflection, and deepening your understanding of yourself and life's mysteries. The energy favors solitude, study, meditation, and any practice that develops inner wisdom. This is not a year for aggressive external action or major new projects. Instead, it's about integrating the lessons of the previous six years and preparing for the culmination ahead.
7.guidance: Turn inward and prioritize your spiritual development. This is your year for meditation, contemplation, study, and inner work. Seek solitude and quiet time. This is not a social year - reduce obligations and protect your alone time. Study subjects that interest your mind and soul. Engage teachers or therapies that support your growth. Trust your intuition more than external advice. This is an excellent year for writing, research, or any solitary creative work. Rest more than usual - this is a fallow period before the harvest. Quality over quantity in all areas.
avoid: Forcing external action or trying to make big things happen
Excessive socializing or filling every moment with activity
Ignoring your intuition in favor of others' advice
Starting major new projects - this is a year to complete and integrate
Materialism or focusing excessively on external success
Isolation to the point of depression - solitude should be rejuvenating

monthlyFocus.early: Establish contemplative practices and protect alone time
monthlyFocus.middle: Deepen study and inner work on subjects calling to you
monthlyFocus.late: Integrate insights and prepare for the culmination ahead

7.affirmation: I trust my inner wisdom and allow time for spiritual growth and integration.

8.energy: Manifestation, achievement, financial reward, recognition, power, material success
8.description: This is your year of manifestation and material reward. After the inner work of Year 7, Year 8 brings your efforts into tangible form and material success. This is the harvest year - you reap what you've sown over the previous seven years. The energy emphasizes achievement, financial gain, recognition, and stepping into your power. This is typically the most materially successful year of the cycle. Opportunities for advancement, increased income, and recognition for your work are likely. The key is handling power and abundance with integrity.
8.guidance: Step into your power and claim the rewards of your efforts. This is your year for advancement, achievement, and material success. Be ambitious - set big goals and take confident action toward them. This is an excellent year for financial matters, business growth, career advancement, and gaining recognition. Handle money and power with integrity. Be willing to be visible and claim your authority. Invest in your success. This is the time to scale up, expand, and achieve at the highest level. Balance material focus with spiritual grounding.
avoid: Playing small or downplaying your accomplishments
Greed or corruption by power and money
Workaholism at the expense of relationships and health
Giving your power away or underselling yourself
Spending beyond your means despite increased income
Using power to dominate or control others

monthlyFocus.early: Set ambitious goals for achievement and financial growth
monthlyFocus.middle: Take powerful action and seize opportunities for advancement
monthlyFocus.late: Handle success with gratitude and integrity

8.affirmation: I claim my power and manifest abundant success with integrity.

9.energy: Endings, completion, letting go, transformation, humanitarian focus, wisdom
9.description: This is your year of completion and release. Year 9 is the final year of the cycle, a time when things naturally come to completion and you're asked to let go of what no longer serves your evolution. This year often brings endings - of relationships, jobs, living situations, or ways of being that have run their course. While this can feel difficult, these endings create space for the new cycle beginning next year. The energy emphasizes completion, releasing, and humanitarian service. Your focus expands beyond personal concerns to embrace larger contribution to humanity.
9.guidance: Complete and release. This is your year to finish projects, end relationships or situations that have run their course, and let go of anything you've outgrown. Don't start major new projects - this is a year for completion and preparation for the new cycle ahead. Clean house literally and metaphorically. Forgive and release old grievances. This is an excellent year for humanitarian service and contribution beyond your personal life. Be generous and give back. Practice non-attachment. Trust that what leaves creates space for something better.
avoid: Clinging to relationships, jobs, or situations past their expiration
Starting major new projects - save those for next year
Bitterness about endings rather than gratitude for lessons learned
Self-focus at the expense of larger contribution
Holding grudges or refusing to forgive
Trying to force things to continue that are meant to end

monthlyFocus.early: Identify what needs to be completed or released
monthlyFocus.middle: Do the work of letting go and forgiving
monthlyFocus.late: Clear space and prepare for the new cycle beginning with Year 1

9.affirmation: I complete with grace, release with gratitude, and trust the cycle of renewal.
